name: PyMC
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); community project; no API costs

performance.notes: MCMC sampling can take minutes to hours; NUTS sampler efficient for most models; JAX/PyTensor backends enable GPU

agent_readiness.notes: Probabilistic programming library for Bayesian statistical modeling. Declarative model specification. MCMC samplers (NUTS, Metropolis), variational inference (ADVI), and Sequential Monte Carlo. ArviZ integration for posterior analysis. PyTensor/JAX backends for GPU acceleration. Used in scientific research, finance, and A/B testing analysis with uncertainty quantification. Gold standard for Bayesian ML in Python.

gotchas: Requires Bayesian statistics knowledge — not for ML beginners
MCMC can be slow for large datasets (use variational inference for scale)
Debugging divergences and convergence requires expertise
best_when: Bayesian regression, hierarchical models, and causal inference
A/B testing analysis with proper uncertainty quantification
Time series analysis with Bayesian structural models
avoid_when: Standard ML prediction tasks (use sklearn/XGBoost for better performance)
Large-scale datasets (MCMC doesn't scale; use deep learning or batch VI)
summary: PyMC is Python's gold standard for Bayesian statistical modeling and probabilistic programming. Declarative model syntax, NUTS/MCMC samplers, variational inference, and ArviZ integration for posterior analysis. JAX backend enables GPU-accelerated sampling. Best for uncertainty-aware modeling, hierarchical models, and causal inference where prediction intervals matter as much as point estimates.
